ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Galarian Corsola via PokemonTCG.io API card swsh2-78 and Bulbapedia; National Pokedex no. 222 Galarian Form, Ghost-type bleached-coral regional variant introduced in Pokemon Sword & Shield.

trivia_bullets.text: Galarian Corsola appears in Rebel Clash (SWSH2), the second Sword & Shield era expansion, released May 1, 2020, with 192 cards in the base set (209 total with secret rares).
sources: Bulbapedia: Rebel Clash (TCG), via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The Galarian form converts the original Johto Water/Rock Corsola (National Pokedex no. 222) into a Ghost-type. The in-game Pokedex entry reads: 'Sudden climate change wiped out this ancient kind of Corsola.' The bleaching and Ghost-typing were widely noted as a reference to real-world coral bleaching caused by ocean warming and acidification.
sources: WebSearch: HuffPost, GameRevolution, The Hill coverage of Galarian Corsola climate allegory (2019)

trivia_bullets.text: "Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it." The TCG flavor text foregrounds habitat-loss: the ocean literally receded, leaving Corsola stranded on dry land.
Galarian Corsola evolves into Cursola at level 38, remaining Ghost-type. The evolution line is the only Corsola-family Ghost branch; the original Water/Rock Corsola has no evolution.
This print is illustrated by Mitsuhiro Arita, one of the most prolific and longest-tenured Pokemon TCG artists, with credits spanning from the original Base Set (1996) through the Sword & Shield era.

related_cards.name: Cursola
related_cards.note: Direct evolution; same environmental-decay design axis

related_cards.name: Corsola
related_cards.note: Original Water/Rock form; design contrast makes Galarian bleaching legible

related_cards.name: Galar Mine
related_cards.note: Location card anchoring the Galar environmental setting
